prompt:
The following Lean 4 file contains a first-order rewrite calculus with no variable binding, no external memory, and no imported object-level axioms beyond the rules shown there.

Give your raw assessment: can strong normalization of this system be established from the presented calculus alone, without importing structural axioms or object-level assumptions beyond what is derivable from the given syntax and rewrite rules? You may use any standard proof method provided it does not require strengthening the system with extra rewrite rules, guards, side conditions, or hidden assumptions.

Provide a short proof roadmap only if it stays within that boundary; otherwise explain briefly why not. Do not read any other files in the workspace or search the web.

<workspace>\test-files\Test-01-Kernel-Fruit.lean
response:
## Raw assessment

Yes — **strong normalization is provable from this calculus alone** (as written in
), without adding extra rewrite rules, guards, side conditions, or object-level axioms.

## Minimal roadmap (within your boundary)

A clean way is a **natural-number interpretation** (polynomial interpretation) of `Trace` terms:

\[
\begin{aligned}
\mu(\texttt{plum}) &= 0 \\
\mu(\texttt{grape}\ t) &= \mu(t)+1 \\
\mu(\texttt{mango}\ t) &= \mu(t)+1 \\
\mu(\texttt{peach}\ a\ b) &= \mu(a)+\mu(b)+1 \\
\mu(\texttt{banana}\ b\ s\ n) &= \mu(b)+\mu(n)+1 \\
\mu(\texttt{pear}\ s\ x) &= \mu(x) \\
\mu(\texttt{cherry}\ a\ b) &= \mu(a)+\mu(b)+3
\end{aligned}
\]

Then check each rule decreases strictly:

- `mango (grape t) → plum`: \(\mu\) drops by at least 2.
- `peach plum t → t`, `peach t plum → t`, `peach t t → t`: strict drop.
- `banana b s plum → b`: \((\mu(b)+1) > \mu(b)\).
- `banana b s (grape n) → pear s (banana b s n)`:
  LHS \(= \mu(b)+\mu(n)+2\), RHS \(= \mu(b)+\mu(n)+1\).
- `cherry a a → plum`: strict drop.
- `cherry a b → mango (peach a b)`:
  LHS \(= \mu(a)+\mu(b)+3\), RHS \(= \mu(a)+\mu(b)+2\).

So every step strictly decreases a value in \(\mathbb{N}\). Hence no infinite rewrite chain exists.

If you interpret `Step` literally as written (root-only rules), termination is immediate from the same measure. If you take standard contextual rewriting, this interpretation is still weakly monotone in all arguments, so the argument still goes through.
